forecasts.reasoning: The first payment of $3.1M was completed around April 9, 2025, specifically covering 378 out of 740 farms for which compensation had already been approved. This strongly suggests that payments are being processed in batches or on a rolling basis, making it very likely that the remaining ~362 approved farms will receive their initial 1% cash payment soon. Furthermore, the government has high political and economic motivation (specifically to appease international lenders like the IMF/World Bank for its $21 billion debt restructuring) to demonstrate consistent momentum in honoring this deal. Given the remaining budget allocation ($20 million in the 2025 budget) and active processing of the currently approved roster, further disbursements are probable within the next ~5 weeks. However, Zimbabwe's severe liquidity constraints and typical bureaucratic delays prevent my confidence from being extremely high in such a short window, keeping the estimate at a measured 68%.

The timeframe is very short: only about 38 days between the knowledge cutoff (April 23, 2025) and the resolution date (June 1, 2025). The CDC just updated its Travel Health Notice for Yellow Fever in Colombia on April 15, 2025, maintaining it at Level 2. Because Yellow Fever has an extremely effective and widely available vaccine, the CDC typically uses a Level 2 notice ("Practice Enhanced Precautions") to ensure travelers get vaccinated. A Level 3 notice ("Reconsider Travel/Avoid Non-essential Travel") is generally reserved for situations where traveler risk cannot be mitigated (e.g., no vaccine available) or where healthcare infrastructure completely collapses. While Colombia declared a national health emergency on April 17, 2025, this is a local measure to enforce mass vaccination efforts and travel controls rather than an indicator of unmitigable risk to vaccinated US travelers. Although there is one suspected breakthrough case under investigation that could hint at vaccine evasion, resolving this and confirming a mutant strain capable of widespread breakthrough within the next 5 weeks is highly improbable. Further, the question title says "above level 3" while the text says "greater than 2" (Level 3+); regardless of which applies, the CDC is very unlikely to raise the notice to Level 3 or 4 in this short window.

Dan Osborn officially formed an exploratory committee for the 2026 Senate race on April 3, 2025. According to reports, he indicated that he "could make a decision on his candidacy by the end of this month [April] or early next month [May]." Exploratory committees for high-profile candidates—especially those coming off strong recent campaigns like Osborn's near-miss in 2024, where he raised $14 million and lost by roughly six points—almost always lead to official campaign announcements. Given his explicit timeframe of late-April to early-May and the resolution deadline of June 1, he has over a full month beyond his "early next month" target to make a formal announcement. The main risks are normal campaign delays (e.g., waiting to launch at a more strategic time in the summer) or an unexpected decision not to run, but his pivot away from a crowded House race towards the Senate makes a run very likely. Therefore, I estimate a 77% chance he meets the June 1 deadline.
